dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal uses 'previous period return is negative' which maps to returns < 0. Exit condition was AUTHOR_SILENT, so a reasonable reversal is implemented: exit when returns turn positive (mean reversion completed). The 'mean-reverting behavior' requirement cannot be quantified without explicit threshold/half-life parameters (marked AUTHOR_SILENT), so entry relies on the negative return condition alone. Target regime set to RANGING as mean reversion is most effective in non-trending environments.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal uses 'previous period return is negative' which maps to returns < 0. Exit condition was AUTHOR_SILENT, so a reasonable reversal is implemented: exit when returns turn positive (mean reversion completed). The 'mean-reverting behavior' requirement cannot be quantified without explicit threshold/half-life parameters (marked AUTHOR_SILENT), so entry relies on the negative return condition alone. Target regime set to RANGING as mean reversion is most effective in non-trending environments.

source_rule_text: Trade stocks exhibiting mean reversion by taking long positions when previous period returns are negative, betting on price recovery.
